title: Occupation is a second game mode
discovery: Once territory is controlled, the simulation should shift into administration, patrol, curfew, requisition, and civilian management.
evidence: source_library/44_military_government.md:15-17 - military government covers temporary authority, curfews, checkpoints, requisition, and command responsibility.
source_library/45_military_occupation.md:15-17 - occupation defines how control is established and how authority behaves in occupied space.
source_library/51_office_of_military_government_united_states.md:15-17 - occupation brings denazification and administrative burden.
source_library/54_military_police_corps_united_states.md:15-17 - military police keep routes, prisoners, and rear areas functioning.
why: Victory should open a different loop, not terminate the simulation.

variable.name: occupation_load
variable.definition: Administrative and security burden created by controlled territory.

mechanic: Post-combat governance layer with patrols, paperwork, and legitimacy management.
pressure: Captured ground keeps demanding attention and manpower.
